title: Pipeline server: skipped steps + directive history
description: Fix skipped-step bug and expose directive history via WebSocket

in: Fix buildPipelineFromDirective to keep skipped steps
Bundle history into directive_updated WS event

tasks.title: Preserve skipped pipeline steps instead of filtering them out
tasks.user_scenario: When viewing a lightweight directive pipeline, user sees all steps with skipped ones greyed out instead of a confusing subset.
tasks.scope: In directive-watcher.ts, change buildPipelineFromDirective from .filter() to .map(), assigning status=skipped. Update both type files.

dod.criterion: buildPipelineFromDirective uses .map() instead of .filter(), returning all pipeline steps
Steps in SKIPPED_STEPS set get status='skipped' in the returned array
PipelineStepStatus in both server/types.ts and src/stores/types.ts includes 'skipped'
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Expose all directives via directiveHistory in WS payload
tasks.user_scenario: Dashboard shows completed and failed directives alongside active ones, giving the CEO full work history.
tasks.scope: Extend DirectiveWatcher to scan all directive directories. Build directiveHistory array with id, title, status, weight, timestamps. Add mtime-based caching. Bundle into directive_updated WS event. Update aggregator, types, WS handler, dashboard store.

dod.criterion: DirectiveWatcher exposes history for all directives regardless of status
Each history entry includes id, title, status, weight, and timestamps
History uses mtime-based cache invalidation (no full rescan on every push)
directive_updated WS payload includes both directiveState and directiveHistory
server/types.ts and src/stores/types.ts updated in lockstep
npm run type-check passes with zero errors
buildPipelineFromDirective returns all steps including skipped ones with status=skipped
DirectiveWatcher exposes directiveHistory array via WS for all directives
npm run type-check passes with zero errors
